Questions.Explanation: Compromise/reconcile The project manager wants to avoid unnecessary risks to the current project by changing the process three weeks from completion, but agrees to implement the process improvement on future projects. The project manager used compromise/reconcile to bring some degree of satisfaction to all parties in order to partially resolve the conflict. The other answer choices are incorrect. There is limited time to accommodate and collaborate. Force/Direct is usually enforced through a power position to resolve an emergency, which is not the case here.

Questions.Text: At a review meeting, project manager learns about unclear requirements that may impact project’s scheduled completion. Project sponsor still wants to meet the original deadline. How should project manager approach the issue using agile practices?
Options: Recommend delivery in multiple releases, focusing on the known scope and important functionalities.
Inform the sponsor that a change request for scope will be added to next iteration
Log unclear scope as an issue and escalate it to product owner for a decision on unclear requirements.
Ask scrum master to clarify scope before continuing the project.
Questions.Explanation: Recommend delivery in multiple releases, focusing on the known scope and important functionalities. The project manager should recommend delivery in multiple releases, focusing on the known scope and important functionalities. Large or vague requirements need to be refined before being included to iteration backlog. Unclear requirements calls for bringing team and product owner together to clarify expectations and value of requirement that likely will delay project. Recommending a release plan to deliver the most important functionality within the original timelines is the best approach for the project manager. Only after clarification, new requirements may be brought to iteration or product backlog. Neither scrum master nor project sponsor is responsible to clarify the requirements.

Questions.Text: At the beginning of a project, a stakeholder is concerned that the initiative may not deliver value to the organization. What should the project manager do to increase the chances of project success?
Options: Review the business case and benefits management plan.
Provide continual communication of the project status to stakeholders.
Schedule training for everyone on the use of the new product.
Perform an audit to make sure the benefits are realized.
Questions.Explanation: Review the business case and benefits management plan. Understanding the business case at the beginning of the project will help the project manager focus on developing a plan that will focus on ensuring that project benefits can be met. The other answer choices may come later.

Questions.Text: A complex, multimillion-dollar, multi-year product development project requiring unique knowledge is 70% complete. Contract termination is a possibility due to project deliverable and execution being not acceptable. Despite strained relationship the vendor is ready for rework at no penalty. What should the project manager do to manage this situation?
Options: Ask the customer to permit rework as the vendor has unique knowledge of the product.
Finalize the new definition of done (DoD) with the customer and make the vendor bear the cost of rework.
Facilitate the negotiation of the acceptance criteria between the customer and vendor.
Direct the vendor to rework the deliverable since they did not meet the requirements.
Questions.Explanation: Facilitate the negotiation of the acceptance criteria between the customer and vendor. In this scenario, the needs of both parties have to be met. The customer's product is a unique one which the vendor has good knowledge about. The vendor has completed 70% of the project and terminating the contract would be a huge loss. Hence, the project manager should facilitate the negotiation of the acceptance criteria between the customer and vendor.

Questions.Text: An innovative project is using artificial intelligence (AI) to identify chemical compounds for the development of new products. The project must deliver at least one product specification within two months and has a fixed budget that cannot be increased. Given that the duration of the AI processing can last from three days to two weeks, and that the AI findings cannot be predicted, how should the project manager maximize the business value delivered with the remaining budget?
Options: Ensure that the specification iterations are limited to 50% of the overall project duration.
Ensure that the AI processing is limited to 25% of the overall project budget.
Plan an iteration directly after AI processing to analyze and prioritize the development goals.
Plan the specification work as a predictive task with a fixed duration to guarantee value.
Questions.Explanation: Plan an iteration directly after AI processing to analyze and prioritize the development goals. Incremental prioritization will ensure that business value can be fulfilled within the project and for future projects. The project manager should plan an iteration directly after AI processing to analyze and prioritize the development goals is the correct answer. The other answer choices are incorrect because they do not support maximizing business value.
